transcript: Hi! Ned Strange with United Parcel Service. I been tryin’—
Good. We’ve been tryin’ to get through on a parcel delivery that no one answered the buzzer last couple times. Just looking for assurances—
—that you’ll be there, ah, this evening when we reattempt delivery. Parcel’s from Schick Corporation. Connecticut.
—Uh, ‘bout 18-pound parcel. So if you have a dolly or something of that nature, that’d be helpful as well. Or we can just leave it in a safe place if you wanna do payment by phone.
[inhales] Um. [exhales] Okay, when would you guys be able to reattempt delivery?
Uh, could I transfer you to my driver and you can maybe work that out with him?
Alex is the driver. One moment, sir. Just a second.
Um. Yeah, I was just transferred over, uh, to see when you guys were gonna be able to reattempt delivery.
I’m sorry, to see what? Say it again?
Yeah, someone, uh—was it “Ned”?—transferred me over to see when you guys were gonna reattempt delivery to, uh, Schick Corporation. I hope you guys—

—No. You’ve got the wrong number. Somebody’s screwing with the phones; they do this all the time. They f—they cross-connect people who don’t know anything about each other and they just mess with us, so you’ll have to bear with it. They’ll probably try again once or twice. And just hang up on ‘em.
Hello, sir? Did we work that out or…?
Yes, this is Winchell with United Parcel Service. How are we today, sir?
Oh, good! Well, we’ve been out there three times and no one answered the buzzer when our driver was there. Any particular reason no one was there for your delivery—on your COD item? [delay & pitch]
“COD”? What are you talking about, sir?
It’s from pullups.com. And we’ve got a parcel—COD $86.81—we’ve been trying to deliver for the past six, seven days. [exhales] [sfx]
I don’t—I don’t buy Pull-Ups, sir.
Uh, well it’s none of my concern whatcha do, where ya order from. I’m just trying to get your parcel to you. Uh, do you know Wendell, our driver?

‘Kay, ‘cause we’re sending him back out there one more time. If no one’s available, we’ll just be returning the item at that juncture. So, [delay & pitch] what is the best time to be there tonight? Mm?
I—I’m not gonna tell you a time unless you tell me—
Pardon me, sir? I mean, this is our fourth attempt; can you appreciate what that means? [delay & pitch]
I appreciate what that means. I think that I should be informed—
—as to who this is delivering for—if you know my name, if you know who ordered this, and, you know, the address—because that would be helpful!
Oh, you placed the order; there’s no doubt in my mind about that. And it’s $88.68 [inhales] [sfx]
I’m on the dock, sir. I’m here on my day off tryin’ to get these packages where they belong [exhales] [sfx]
I’m sorry, I’m gonna need more information, ‘cause I don’t know what you’re talking about, exactly.
Okay, pullups.com—some kinda garment. Uh, $86.68 is due—cash, check. Uh, we can take a credit card now and you can tell us where to leave the item. It’s a big, big box.
Hi, yes! This is Sampson with UPS calling—uh, we’re in need of a credit card for your order, sir?
[continual sfx] You did not order anything?
And, uh, here’s the thing, fucker. If you call again, I’m gonna call the cops on you for scamming people.
Well we’re on our way out there with your box, uh—uh, this evening, here!—
—Oh! Well what—what—what’s the address?! What’s the address, Charlie?
—Suite 3600. And my name’s Sampson with UPS.com. [inhales]
Cool! But we did not order from there—
—No, your order is from pullups.com. Okay? [delay & pitch]—
—Yeah, but we did not—I’m telling you we did not order them!
We just need a payment tonight for $86.78. M’kay? [deay & pitch]—
Hello! This is Elmer. I’m a supervisor at UPS.com. Uh—
—what appears to be the trouble up here, sir?
[mimicking LPC’s pitch-shifted vocals] I’m a supervisor!
Fuck, dude! What the fuck? Stop fuckin’ calling me, now! Seriously! Stop! Fuck off!—
—Uh, sir, I’m Elmer Blip. I’m a manager at UPS—
—I give a fuck what you are, dude! Fuck you! Stop fuckin’ calling me!—
—We’ve got a COD for ya on our truck, okay? [delay]
—What appears to be the trouble up here, sir? We’ve got a parcel for you we’ve been trying to deliver. No one answered the door—
—Hey, I didn’t order anything. Quit callin’. I didn’t order—
—You didn’t order anything? We’ve got a parcel here f—your signature on it right here! I just need to know the best time o’ day to bring it to ya.
Yeah, I didn’t order anything. [unclear] quit callin’ me—
I can be there in 20 minutes there, guy.
You’ve called me from three different numbers—
—We’ll be there in 15, 20 minutes, you got that? COD.
—And I’d like it taken care of today. I don’t want any problems with our driver when he gets there; is that understood? [exhales]
Uh. You can drop it off somewhere else. It’s not mine.
“Somewhere else”? Not an option, sir! This is your item! COD $48.84—
—Alright. Uh, I didn’t order a product. By—
—“88-inch whip” is what it says it is, here—
Are you hard o’ hearing? Whip!—
—I have no idea. W—quit calling! I know this is fake so, like, whoever’s behind it—
—This is UPS, sir. I’m in the corporate tower of UPS, here. Alright? And I don’t want any fiddlin’ around when our driver comes back!—
—Well earlier, you said “United Postal Service”. So I—
—[unclear] Oh! [snickers] Oh! You’re mistaken [snickers] Quit callin’.
Who knows where I work, me or you?
Look, I’m ‘bout to report this number if you don’t quit callin’, for real—
—Well, sir, I’m from United Parcel Service—
—I’ve got work to do. This is getting fuckin’ annoying!—
—We’ve got a delivery to make, here. We need you to cut a check to our driver!
You don’t cut a check to—
—[breaks up] be from the company in which he got ordered for. So—
—We’re tryin’ ta deliver. This is our fourth attempt, sir.
Hold onto it! ‘Cause I didn’t order anything, I’m tellin’ you! I’m telling you I didn’t order anything—
—We’ve been holding on to it for a week!—
Well then how come I haven’t gotten a receipt or a letter sayin’ that—come pick it up?
Oh, we sent you an email, we left ya a voicemail.
Oh, huh! Well, that’s a funny thing [unclear] but I keep checkin’ my email and voicemail ‘cause I have a job that you do not [unclear]—
—Oh, exactly! Shut the fuck up and quit callin’ me. I’m gettin’ tired of—I’m ‘bout to report this number to the police. [quietly] So fucking quit calling me! [unclear]—
—I’m about to call the police on you for threatening a UPS representative.
Now how you gon’ like that?! [exhales]—
—All I said I was gonna do is call the cops!
You got a whip [exhales] we’re tryin’ to deliver. I’d like it done today!—
—How ‘bout I get that whip and beat the fuck out of you with it? Quit callin’ me!
I got a stick I’ll bring up there.
Yes. This is Fabio with UPS. Our driver’s been out there four times recently and no one answered—
—ah, his call for a COD delivery.
—Uh, we’ve been tryin ta make a COD delivery. That means “cash on delivery”—
Says “contents are Sequoian pellets” [emergency siren sfx]
Yeah, I don’t know what that is—
—So! [delay & pitch] I hear that all the time!
This is Fabio with UPS. I’ve got your pellets for delivery—
—I don’t wanna be fuckin’ woken up! Leave me the fuck alone!—
How fucking stupid are you?! Do not fucking call me!—
This is UPS.com. I’m looking for Mi—
Sequoian pellets! you’re gonna love what ya see!—
—You called me four times today! Leave me alone!
Our driver’s on his way back there, ma’am!—
—Do not call me again! I will call the fucking cops! Stop harassing me! Leave me the fuck alone!
Oh! Can I talk to Pat, please?
What part of “leave me alone” don’t you understand?—
—Uh, this is Max with UPS, ma’am. I’m tryin’ to reach Pat about your package—
—Oh, oh! Okay! Okay! Okay! I’m sorry. I’m sorry, I’ve g—had so many scam calls! Alright, what about ya?
A spam call? No, no, no! United Parcel Service, ma’am.
And we’ve been out there three times in the past 10 days, our driver says, and no one answered the door! Up ‘ere!
Well, he didn’t knock very hard, then! And why did you need—a—usually, they just leave it on the porch.
Well this is a COD order. $76.81.
It’s from The Fungal Brothers. “Feed ’n Seed”, it says.
28 pounds, Feed ’n Seed, Fungal Brothers. Yes, ma’am!—
I’m lookin’ at your signature and the—we’ve already been out there three times, ma’am! [piano note]
—Now what’s the best time to come back up ‘ere? We can be—
We’ll be there at, uh, 10 o’clock tonight. Alright? [delay & pitch] Is that alright with you?—
—10 o’—? No! no! Not no 10 o’clock at night!
No, no, no, no?! [pitch-shifted] Ma’am, can you appreciate what we’ve done for ya? We’ve been out there three times in the past 10 days with your bag o’ seed, here!
But I did not order nothin’!—
—Oh! [delay & pitch] You did not order anything?
—Well, I’ve got the address and I’m lookin’ at your signature, right here. I’m Maximillian, okay? From United Parcel Service. Have you got a check prepared?—
You do not? [delay] Well, how long’s it gonna take—
Well, I’m sorry. I’m not payin’ you no $86.
I can take a credit card over the phone and leave the bag in a safe place; that’s no problem at all!—
—Good—good for you—good for you! But no! Thank you!—
—“No”? Just read the credit card number slowly. [birds]
Uh, he is busy right now. Who’s calling?
Uh, this is, uh, Doc with United Parcel Service.
Yeah? And what is it about?
Uh, it’s about his COD parcel ma’am.
Mhm. Well, I’m his wife, so what can I help you with?
Oh, okay! I see! Yeah, uh-huh! [delay]
—He has a parcel that’s cash on delivery?
[sfx] From “Batista Morocco”, it says on the label—
I don’t know who that is so don’t deliver it, ‘cause I’m—we’re not gonna pay for it.
Well our truck’s already on the way ma’am.
—And he’s been out there twice but no one answered the door of all things, huh? [sfx] [slowed]
Alright! I’ll listen for the door again. I—
—Okay. Have you got a check prepared?—
—I never leave the house—Y—I’m not gonna pay for anything until I see what it is. Thank you.
Doc from United Parcel Service. Now, just tryin’ to find—er out what the problem is up ‘ere. [exhales]
Yeah, okay, look, I’ve never had a b—a phone call from United—from UPS—
—So, if you have something to deliver—
—knock on the door and please don’t call me back again—
—We’ve been out there three times. No one answered the buzzer up ‘ere! No how do you go figure that? What would you like us to do?
Excuse me, will you please hang up the phone? What do I have to do to disconnect your number? I said—
—W—can I get a credit card number? We can leave it in a safe place! That’s okay—
—No! No! There’s no credit card number—
—on the phone. There’s no credit card number on the phone. I don’t know who you are.
I’m Doc with UPS.com, okay? In the State—
—No, I don’t—I’m—no. I—I don’t believe you—
Yeah, well, good for [breaks up]. This sounds like a scam to me. I don’t give out my—my credit card and I don’t write checks to people I don’t know.
—If someone shows up at my door and they have a plausib— [breaks up]
—These are like f—75 pounds of beans—taking up a lotta space in our truck!—that you ordered—
I’ve got your signature right here, ma’am.
—Just cut a check and then we’ll drop it off in a safe place. Hello? [delay] I’m a manager—
—Could you not call? No, I’m not interested.
—Oh, come on, Doc! No, not interested!—
Hello? Hello, may I help you?—
Hi! Um, this is Howard from UPS. So I’ll be assisting your very concern about your package, ma’am.
Look, if you have a UPS delivery, deliver it to my house.
Yeah. Okay. Um, do you have, like, a tracking number? [unclear, perhaps, “four-to-five day mail” for me to erase this?
I don’t have—I haven’t ordered anything through UPS, so…
—I did not—Alright? I don’t know—Doc is calling me—I think it’s a scam. So, please don’t call me—
—Yeah, this might be a scam, ma’am. Okay? Thank you! Thank you!
